# Fake Email

> Free, anonymous disposable email service. Generate a temporary inbox in one click — no signup, no tracking. Built for developers, signups, and email verification testing. REST API available.

## Summary

Fake Email is a free temporary email (temp mail) service hosted at https://fake-email.site. Users (or agents) create a disposable mailbox at `@fake-email.site` and receive incoming messages in real time. Mailboxes auto-expire; nothing persists. No personal data is collected. The backend is an open-source Rust SMTP and HTTP service.

## Primary URLs

- Home: https://fake-email.site/
- Inbox UI: https://fake-email.site/emails
- API documentation (human): https://fake-email.site/docs/api
- OpenAPI 3.1 spec: https://fake-email.site/openapi.json
- Health endpoint: https://fake-email.site/api/health

## Agent discovery

- Link header on `/` advertises: api-catalog, agent-skills, service-doc, service-desc, status
- API catalog (RFC 9727): https://fake-email.site/.well-known/api-catalog
- Agent Skills index (RFC v0.2.0): https://fake-email.site/.well-known/agent-skills/index.json
- Markdown content negotiation: send `Accept: text/markdown` to receive a markdown variant of the HTML page

## Core API

POST https://fake-email.site/api/temporary-address
  Body (optional): {"username": "alice"}
  Response: {"temp_email_addr": "alice@fake-email.site"}

GET https://fake-email.site/api/inbox/poll?address=<addr>
  Response: {"messages": [...]}

GET https://fake-email.site/api/health
  Response: {"status": "ok"}

## Features

- Instant disposable email address generation
- Real-time inbox polling
- No registration, no captcha, no tracking
- Open-source backend (Rust)
- REST API + OpenAPI spec
- Auto-expiring mailboxes

## Use cases

- Sign up for services without exposing a real address
- Receive one-time verification codes or password resets
- QA / CI automation for email-dependent flows
- Avoid spam after a known leak

## Repository

https://github.com/Shivrajsoni/fake-email

## License

Open-source. Free to use.
